Game over with score 0 - failed level 1. Board reset to initial state. Let me restart with a cleaner approach.
World model (reset):
- Initial state: character (white+red) at top-left, green container at bottom-right

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if game has reset - look at current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current state:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min_r}-{max_r} cols={min_c}-{max_c}")

# The game should have reset. Let me try a direct approach:
# Move character RIGHT and DOWN to reach green container
# Character is at rows 11-25, cols 11-25
# Green container is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49
# Need to move right ~20 and down ~10 to overlap

# First, let me try moving right to approach the green container
result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter 10 RIGHT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min_r}-{max_r} cols={min_c}-{max_c}")
green_total = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
print(f"Green: {green_total}, completed: {result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
